trivia_bullets.text: Undergrowth Scavenger is a common from Magic 2015 (M15), released July 18, 2014. M15 was the first core set to credit individual card designers and introduced the card frame used through 2022. [Scryfall]

Type line is 'Creature — Fungus Horror' (not Insect as the visual art implies). Fungus Horror is a rare compound subtype; the Fungus designation places the creature in decay-ecology rather than arthropod taxonomy, consistent with its graveyard-counting mechanic. [Scryfall]

Enters as a 0/0 sized by creature cards in ALL players' graveyards. A ruling notes that if Undergrowth Scavenger itself enters from a graveyard (via reanimation), it counts itself when determining ETB counters. EDHREC rank 18,447. [Scryfall, MTGAssist via WebSearch]
"It sees a rotting carcass as a good wine which has been aged properly." [Scryfall] — unattributed. Reframes decay as connoisseurship for a creature whose fitness depends on the dead.

Illustrated by Nils Hamm, one of the most prolific MTG artists of the 2010s with approximately 195 card credits [Gatherer via WebSearch]. Hamm frequently works in dark-organic and horror-adjacent registers.

related_cards.name: Lhurgoyf
related_cards.note: progenitor of 'size = creature cards in graveyard' designs

related_cards.name: Mortivore
related_cards.note: counts all graveyards permanently (not just on ETB), regenerates

related_cards.name: Splinterfright
related_cards.note: counts cards in your own graveyard; self-mill variant
